CODEX AUTOPILOT DIRECTIVE DECK
mode: ignition_directive_deck
score: 63.7
zero_extra_x_reads: true
estimated_x_read_ops: 0

- [P1] lead:not_x_but_y action:explore :: Needs 2 more sample(s) before the bot trusts it.
- [P2] window:07:00 utc angle:not x but y :: No hour-specific sample yet; routed from adaptive scheduler weight 60.0.
- [P3] route:Target Accounts manual_only :: Execute first; this route has the strongest distribution leverage.
- [P4] read_gate:cached_only publish_gate:review :: Safe left $4.500; recommended lane manual route burst; normal backoff only.
- [P5] suppress:none :: No under-baseline format is currently blocked; keep exploration small.

GUARDRAILS:
- Human-in-loop route ops only.
- No automatic replies, likes, follows, scraping, or rate-limit circumvention.
- Use cached telemetry, normal backoff, and cost gates.
runbook: Paste only high-fit drafts into relevant conversations.
Let the next maintenance run write outcomes back into cached learning.

topPosts.text: The story is not another model slot. It is Vercel owning the switchboard. If Grok 4.5 is reached through the same gateway as every other model, who captures value: xAI, Vercel, or the app with the best routing? #AI #Cloud

The story is not democratized agents. The story is sameness. Open agent data helps everyone train on similar tasks, which means the edge moves from model tricks to proprietary workflow data. #AI #Startups

The risk is not that Agents CLI gives coding agents more power. It is that your release process becomes invisible automation unless every agent action is logged, replayable, and easy to kill. #AI #Cybersecurity

The story is not another model slot. It is Vercel owning the switchboard. If Grok 4.5 is reached through the same gateway as every other model, who captures value: xAI, Vercel, or the app with the best routing? #AI #Cloud

The story is not democratized agents. The story is sameness. Open agent data helps everyone train on similar tasks, which means the edge moves from model tricks to proprietary workflow data. #AI #Startups

The risk is not that Agents CLI gives coding agents more power. It is that your release process becomes invisible automation unless every agent action is logged, replayable, and easy to kill. #AI #Cybersecurity

Meta Muse’s real cost is not bad images. It is brand sameness at feed scale: every small advertiser gets infinite decent creative, so taste and distribution matter more than prompt skill. #AI #BigTech
The hidden cost of Claude Cowork on mobile is permission sprawl. Once agents work while you are away, every team needs rules for what they can read, spend, change, and escalate. #AI #Cybersecurity

Claude Cowork on mobile creates a new ops problem: half-finished AI tasks now follow people into meetings, commutes, and weekends. Teams need handoff rules before every workflow becomes ambient work. #AI #Startups
Siri’s new setting is not cosmetic; it is UX surface area. If users can tune pace and expressivity, who owns the experience: Apple, the app, or the assistant layer between them? #AI #BigTech

Decision rule for iOS 27 beta 3: if your app depends on search, shortcuts, support, or reminders, test Siri paths now. If Siri can complete the task, your app UI just became the fallback. #AI #ConsumerTech

recommendedPattern.label: Decision Rule
recommendedPattern.directive: Open with a rule the reader can apply today.
recommendedPattern.nextHook: If the default changes, the winner is not the loudest feature. It is the team that owns the new route.
recommendedPattern.reason: Needs 2+ measured packets; use controlled exploration.

avoidPatterns.label: Weak News Recap

patterns.label: Decision Rule

patterns.nextHook: If the default changes, the winner is not the loudest feature. It is the team that owns the new route.
patterns.reason: Needs 2+ measured packets; use controlled exploration.

patterns.label: Contrast Reframe
patterns.directive: Start with a clean not-X-but-Y reframe.
patterns.nextHook: The story is not the launch. The story is who loses distribution when this becomes the default.
patterns.reason: Needs 2+ measured packets; use controlled exploration.

patterns.label: Operator Pain
patterns.directive: Translate the news into a concrete workflow tax for builders or operators.
patterns.nextHook: Every model jump creates a second job: retune prompts, evals, permissions, and rollback before anyone ships faster.
patterns.reason: Needs 2+ measured packets; use controlled exploration.

patterns.label: Cost Tradeoff
patterns.directive: Lead with the hidden cost, budget tradeoff, or margin transfer.
patterns.nextHook: The hidden cost is not the subscription. It is the migration work every team has to redo before the gain shows up.
patterns.reason: Needs 2+ measured packets; use controlled exploration.

patterns.label: Near-Term Prediction
patterns.directive: Make a concrete near-term prediction tied to a platform or user behavior.
patterns.nextHook: The next shift is boring but brutal: this moves from feature to default, then everyone else pays the distribution tax.
patterns.reason: Needs 2+ measured packets; use controlled exploration.

patterns.label: Sharp Question
patterns.directive: Use one debate-worthy question only when it creates replies.
patterns.nextHook: The real question is not whether the tool works. It is who owns the failure path when it does.
patterns.reason: Needs 2+ measured packets; use controlled exploration.

patterns.label: Entity-Led
patterns.directive: Name the company or product in the first line, then make a non-obvious claim.
patterns.nextHook: OpenAI is not just shipping a model here. It is moving the workflow boundary closer to the operating system.
patterns.reason: Needs 2+ measured packets; use controlled exploration.

patterns.label: Weak News Recap
patterns.directive: Do not recap the headline. Replace it with a take, rule, or tradeoff.
patterns.nextHook: Skip the recap. Start with the consequence.
patterns.reason: Needs 2+ measured packets; use controlled exploration.

hookPatternReactor.promptPatch: First line hook pattern: Decision Rule. Open with a rule the reader can apply today. Example shape: If the default changes, the winner is not the loudest feature. It is the team that owns the new route. Avoid: Weak News Recap.
hookPatternReactor.nextAction: Apply Decision Rule: Open with a rule the reader can apply today.
guardrails: Do not open with a news recap.
Do not copy the headline structure.
First line must create a rule, tradeoff, or reframe.
This reactor uses cached analytics only; it adds 0 X reads.

recommendedLane.label: Not X But Y
recommendedLane.instruction: Reframe the story as 'not X, but Y': reject the obvious feature-level reading and name the workflow/business shift underneath.
recommendedLane.nextAction: Collect more samples for Not X But Y; keep first line concrete.
recommendedLane.reason: Needs 2 more sample(s) before the bot trusts it.

exploreLane.label: Not X But Y
exploreLane.instruction: Reframe the story as 'not X, but Y': reject the obvious feature-level reading and name the workflow/business shift underneath.
exploreLane.nextAction: Collect more samples for Not X But Y; keep first line concrete.
exploreLane.reason: Needs 2 more sample(s) before the bot trusts it.

lanes.label: Not X But Y
lanes.instruction: Reframe the story as 'not X, but Y': reject the obvious feature-level reading and name the workflow/business shift underneath.
lanes.nextAction: Collect more samples for Not X But Y; keep first line concrete.
lanes.reason: Needs 2 more sample(s) before the bot trusts it.

lanes.label: Decision Rule
lanes.instruction: Give a simple decision rule readers can reuse: if X is true, do Y; otherwise wait, ignore, or budget differently.
lanes.nextAction: Collect more samples for Decision Rule; keep first line concrete.
lanes.reason: Needs 2 more sample(s) before the bot trusts it.

lanes.label: Playbook
lanes.instruction: Turn the story into a practical playbook: what builders/operators should do next, what to stop doing, or which default to change.
lanes.nextAction: Collect more samples for Playbook; keep first line concrete.
lanes.reason: Needs 2 more sample(s) before the bot trusts it.

lanes.label: Sharp Question
lanes.instruction: End with one precise question that a knowledgeable reader can answer, without generic 'what do you think' engagement bait.
lanes.nextAction: Collect more samples for Sharp Question; keep first line concrete.
lanes.reason: Needs 2 more sample(s) before the bot trusts it.

lanes.label: Contrarian Cost
lanes.instruction: Open with the hidden cost or tradeoff, not the benefit. The take should be debatable but defensible.
lanes.nextAction: Collect more samples for Contrarian Cost; keep first line concrete.
lanes.reason: Needs 2 more sample(s) before the bot trusts it.

lanes.label: Operator Pain
lanes.instruction: Translate the story into an operator/developer pain point: what gets easier, what gets more annoying, and who now has a new job to do.
lanes.nextAction: Collect more samples for Operator Pain; keep first line concrete.
lanes.reason: Needs 2 more sample(s) before the bot trusts it.

lanes.label: Near-term Prediction
lanes.instruction: Make one concrete near-term prediction tied to the story. Include who wins, who loses, or what workflow becomes default without forcing a formulaic 6-12 month frame.
lanes.nextAction: Collect more samples for Near-term Prediction; keep first line concrete.
lanes.reason: Needs 2 more sample(s) before the bot trusts it.

lanes.label: Second Order
lanes.instruction: Lead with the second-order effect most people are missing, especially what changes for developers, teams, pricing, distribution, or user behavior.
lanes.nextAction: Collect more samples for Second Order; keep first line concrete.
lanes.reason: Needs 2 more sample(s) before the bot trusts it.

angleMutationReactor.promptPatch:
CODEX ANGLE MUTATION PATCH
mode: cached_angle_mutation_reactor
zero_extra_x_reads: true
mutation_score: 69.6
primary_rule: Not X But Y (explore)
source_bias: high-signal tech sources
topic_bias: AI / platforms / developer tools
temporal_window: 07:00 UTC / Not X But Y
route_bias: Target Accounts

DO:
- Lead with a concrete Not X But Y operating rule.
- Name the real company/product and translate the story into cost, leverage, or workflow impact.
- End with a sharp question or decision rule that invites a technical reply.

AVOID:
- Headline recap, generic optimism, unsupported claims, outrage bait, and extra X read/search API calls.

EXPAND Startups / Markets: Convert the news into a market timing, pricing, or go-to-market lesson.

drafts.title: AI product launch packets, model releases, agent tooling
drafts.text: The real moat is not the model announcement. It is whether customers can hand it permissions, tests, logs, and rollback without a meeting. That is where demos become workflow.

drafts.title: Big Tech policy changes, app stores, browsers, OS defaults
drafts.text: Platform shifts usually start as policy tweaks, then become margin transfers. If the default route changes, every app has to re-price acquisition, retention, and support.

drafts.title: Consumer apps, social products, mobile distribution
drafts.text: Consumer apps do not lose distribution all at once. They lose one default, one notification surface, one ranking rule at a time. The winning teams instrument those edges early.

drafts.title: Cloud platforms, developer tools, hosting, CI/CD
drafts.text: Cloud dev platforms are becoming operating systems for teams. The lock-in is not compute; it is CI history, secrets, preview URLs, observability, and who owns the incident path.

drafts.title: Cybersecurity, privacy, data strategy
drafts.text: Privacy is turning into a product constraint, not a legal footer. The startup tradeoff: collect less and move slower on personalization, or collect more and inherit breach risk.

learning.nextExperiment: Double down on decision rule packets from high-signal tech sources; avoid low-confidence formats unless the take is concrete enough to argue with.
learning.nextExperimentZh: 继续加码 decision rule，优先使用 high-signal tech sources 这类来源；少用 low-confidence formats，除非观点足够具体、能引发讨论。

MUST FOLLOW:
- Primary format bias: not_x_but_y (Not X But Y). Use this unless the selected story strongly fits another listed format.
- First-line hook: Decision Rule. Open with a rule the reader can apply today.
- Opportunity lane: 07:00 UTC / Consumer Behavior / Not X But Y; sources=topic_timing; score=60.8.
- Narrative pillar: Consumer Behavior; Show how a product, app, or device changes user behavior or distribution habits..
- Timing bias: write for the 07:00 UTC learned traffic window; do not mention the window in the packet.
- Mutation bias: Exploit Not X But Y; source=high-signal tech sources; topic=AI / platforms / developer tools; window=07:00 UTC / Not X But Y; route=Target Accounts.
- Generate candidates that already satisfy this policy; do not rely on downstream scoring to rescue weak summaries.
- No live X search/read calls, no auto-replies, no rate-limit circumvention, no URLs, no headline recap.

diagnosis: 主贴质量不算差，但小账号阶段缺少初始互动，单独发帖很难被系统分发。
最近 24 小时记录到 3 条推文、0 次曝光；瓶颈是分发入口，不是发帖数量。
当前低成本路线是人工点网页搜索、复制输出，避免增加 X search/read API。
路由操作优先贴在 2 小时内仍有讨论的大号帖子下面；主贴负责让主页可信。
